metadata.notes: agent=yes (kiro-cli --model / /model picker / --list-models pin a model); daemon=full as of #599: the parser reads the <uuid>.json sidecar rts_model_state at turn_done (TranscriptPathAware) → ModelName + ContextWindow + derived context utilization; driver=ready (settings.model knob appends --model at launch).

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

Route: record_now — UNFROZEN by #599. The prior assessment (incapable/frozen) was correct for the v1 adapter: the tailed JSONL carries no model field and the sidecar went unread. The #599 sidecar metrics reader closes exactly that gap, so the two user-observable signals now resolve end to end.

## agent_supports — yes (unchanged)

kiro-cli pins a concrete model three ways, confirmed against the live binary: `kiro-cli chat --model <id>` (the recipe path), the in-REPL `/model` picker, and `--list-models` to enumerate ids (e.g. claude-haiku-4.5 at 0.40x credits — the recipe choice).

## daemon_capability — full (as of #599; was incapable)

The tailer injects the transcript path via tailer.TranscriptPathAware at construction; on every text-only AssistantMessage (turn_done) the kirocli parser token-walks the <uuid>.json sidecar to session_state.rts_model_state and sets ModelName (the pinned id passes NormalizeModelName), ContextWindow (from context_window_tokens — no capacity-table lookup needed), and a derived Tokens.Total = context_usage_percentage/100 × window so ComputeContextUtilization reproduces kiro's own number. Live-verified on the 1-8 sibling cell (model-context-display): websocket metrics {model_name auto, context_window 200000, context_utilization 1.24%} on the default launch; this cell is the same pipeline with a PINNED id, so the sidecar's model_id carries the launch model from turn 1. Replay reproduces it because the fixture carries transcript.json (the sidecar) next to transcript.jsonl and the replay engine stages it (#599).

## driver_capability — ready

The driver's settings.model knob (#599) appends `--model <m>` at launch, mirroring the trust_all_tools seam. One send + wait_turn records the cell.
caveats: The pinned id is kiro's OWN label (claude-haiku-4.5, dotted) — NormalizeModelName may pass it through rather than map to a canonical irrlicht id; the scenario asserts non-unknown identity, not capacity-table membership. ContextWindow comes from the sidecar, so no capacity dependency.
Sidecar token counts remain zero (credits-only metering): context utilization is derived from context_usage_percentage; token ACCOUNTING (5.1) stays frozen.
Replay needs transcript.json next to transcript.jsonl in the fixture (engine stages <base>.json — #599).

sources.note: applySidecarMetrics on turn_done: ModelName/ContextWindow from rts_model_state + derived Tokens.Total; path injected via SetTranscriptPath (tailer.TranscriptPathAware, #599).
readSidecarModelState token-walks <base>.json → session_state → rts_model_state, skipping conversation_metadata.
settings.model knob appends --model <m> at launch (mirrors trust_all_tools seam, #599).
Sibling cell model-context-display recorded 3/3 phases; golden summary.model_name = auto. Same pipeline, pinned id here.
Valid pinnable ids incl. claude-haiku-4.5 (0.40x credits, the recipe choice); kiro-cli chat --help shows --model <MODEL>.

preconditions: FROZEN cell — not recordable until the unblock lands: a sidecar MetricsReader reading <uuid>.json session_state.rts_model_state.model_info.{model_id,context_window_tokens} into SessionMetrics.{ModelName,ContextWindow}. Until then a recording would show ModelName='unknown' (the verdict), so do not record.
kiro-cli on PATH and authenticated out-of-band (subscription auth; the TUI persists a session file under ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/, unlike headless --no-interactive which writes none — FINDINGS.md §7).
tmux available — the interactive driver runs the kiro-cli TUI in a tmux REPL.
irrlichd is recording (run-cell isolated mode, or --attach against a --record daemon).
Launch with an EXPLICIT --model <id> so model_info.model_id pins a concrete model (not 'auto') — required for the assertion to be meaningful once the reader exists.
setup: Driver launches `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools --model claude-haiku-4.5` (settings.model knob, #599) so the sidecar rts_model_state carries the pinned id from turn 1
Assertions on ModelName/ContextWindow are checked AFTER the first turn_done (the sidecar read fires on the text-only AssistantMessage)
Curation copies the <uuid>.json sidecar into the cell as transcript.json so replay reproduces the metrics

script.text: What is 6 times 7? Answer with just the number.

verify: after the first turn_done, metrics.model_name = claude-haiku-4.5 (the pinned launch model, not unknown, not auto)
metrics.context_utilization_percentage > 0 and equal to the sidecar context_usage_percentage
final session state is ready; topology ready → working → ready
staged fixture contains transcript.json next to transcript.jsonl
recipe.notes: Pinned-model variant of the 1-8 first-contact check: 1.8 asserts default-launch visibility (model auto), this cell asserts the LAUNCH PIN is identified. claude-haiku-4.5 chosen as the cheapest concrete pin (0.40x credits).
